myths.Agent_1:
Long ago, before the world had voices, the great mountains stood in absolute silence. Among them was Oros, the tallest peak, whose heart was carved from lonely granite. Oros watched the rivers dance and the forests sway, but he could not join their songs. He desired nothing more than to speak, to have his towering presence known to the endless sky.

One twilight, the wandering Wind, Zephyra, swept across his craggy face. Feeling the deep sorrow vibrating within the stone, she paused. "Why do you weep silent dust, old giant?" she whispered. 

Oros could not answer. Moved by his mute isolation, Zephyra made a sacred pact with the earth. She breathed a fragment of her own voice into the deepest caverns of Oros's chest. "I cannot give you words of your own," she declared, "for stone must always remain steadfast. But whenever a voice calls out to you, you shall no longer be silent. You will catch the sound and toss it back to the world."

From that day on, whenever travelers or beasts shouted into the valleys, Oros answered. He repeated their calls with a booming joy, and this magical gift forever became known to all as the Echo.
myths.Agent_2:
Long before humanity first mapped the sprawling green valleys, the great mountain spirit Oros sat in profound silence. Made of granite and frost, he was a solitary stone titan who yearned for companionship. Every night, Oros would rumble a deep, sorrowful song to the stars, but the heavens were too distant to answer.

One evening, a playful wind nymph named Aura fluttered through the jagged peaks. She heard Oros’s mournful melody and felt a twinge of pity. Aura possessed no voice of her own, only the rustle of leaves and the howl of the gale. She could not speak his heavy, earthen language. 

Determined to comfort the lonely giant, Aura caught his booming voice in her silvery cloak as it left his lips. She spun around the canyon walls, carrying the heavy syllables, and threw them back to him. 

"Hello," Oros called out. 

"Hello," Aura tossed back, her breath softening the rough edges of his voice.

Oros smiled warmly, believing the mountains themselves had finally awakened to speak with him. To this day, whenever a wanderer shouts into a deep canyon, Aura playfully catches the words and throws them back, ensuring no one ever feels truly alone in the wild.
